title: Authors and Citation
text: Moffitt N (2026). orderlabel: Order Tables Add Percent Labels. R package version 0.4.3, https://github.com/nick-moffitt/orderlabel.
code: @Manual{,   title = {orderlabel: Order Your Tables and Add Percent Labels},   author = {Nick Moffitt},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.4.3},   url = {https://github.com/nick-moffitt/orderlabel}, }

title: Order Your Tables and Add Percent Labels
text: orderlabel second package Y2 Analytics series. Many functions run heavily frequencies data frame created freqs() function y2clerk package. premier function package order_label(), quickly go major functions well. order_label() - takes frequency table created freqs() orders charting. several key arguments within function allow user easily specify envision ordering type chart table used . example, key ordering arguments “inherent_order_label”, “label_specific”, “group_var”, “stacked”, among others. order_same() - similar order_label, time give function another data frame reference, new data frame ordered way. taking_names() - short function run beginning every project. takes one argument, data frame, create new data frame variable names labels quick reference using ctrl + F search variables. other_rm() - running freqs, add other_rm remove parenthetical additions variable label well “please specify” parts “, please specify” type answers. Trims labels quickly. preamble_rm() - works similar other_rm. time removes question preambles variable labels “favorite color? - Blue”, want “Blue”. functions , sure check !

title: Order Your Tables and Add Percent Labels
text: can install released version orderlabel CRAN : development version GitHub :
code: install.packages("orderlabel") # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("nick-moffitt/orderlabel")

title: Order Your Tables and Add Percent Labels
text: find basic examples show quickly order frequencies table order_label():
code: library(orderlabel) library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union # Ungrouped, put in descending order of the result frequencies <- tibble(   label = c('Brand 1', 'Brand 2', 'Brand 3', 'Brand 4', 'Brand 5'),   result = c(.25, .15, .20, .10, .30),   value = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5) ) |> order_label()  # # Grouped, with an inherent order for the label, or the brand # frequencies <- tibble( #   label = rep(c('Brand 1', 'Brand 2', 'Brand 3', 'Brand 4', 'Brand 5'), 2), #   result = c(.20, .20, .30, .10, .20, .20, .30, .20, .20, .10), #   value = rep(c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5), 2), #   group_var = c(rep('Group A', 5), rep('Group B', 5)) # ) |> order_label( #   group_var = group_var, #   inherent_order_label = T # ) #  # # Stacked, will be using this frequencies in an mschart later on # frequencies <- tibble( #   label = rep(c('Promoter', 'Passive', 'Detractor'), 3), #   result = c(.33, .33, .34, .20, .30, .50, .25, .50, .25), #   value = rep(c(1, 2, 3), 3), #   group_var = c(rep('Group A', 3), rep('Group B', 3), rep('Group C', 3)) # ) |> order_label( #   group_var = group_var, #   stacked = 'ms' # )

title: Order Your Tables and Add Percent Labels
text: issues using orderlabel, please post issue GitHub along minimal reproducible example. best address issues get fixed next version orderlabel.

what: Add age and census age groups to a data frame — add_ages
title: Add age and census age groups to a data frame — add_ages
text: Takes data frame year born variable age variable creates four new variables:

title: Add age and census age groups to a data frame — add_ages
code: add_ages(   dataset,   year_born_var,   age_var,   year_of_survey = 0,   survey_date_var = "NULL" )

title: Add age and census age groups to a data frame — add_ages
text: dataset name data frame function modify, usually piped main data frame year_born_var DFAULT; may either provide year_born_var age_var, age_var DFAULT year_of_survey DEFAULT = Whatever current year . Used calculate respondent ages. using old survey data previous year, can input year numeric value (e.g. 1994) survey_date_var DEFAULT = 'NULL'. date variable dataset specifies survey taken. specified, ages calculated year_of_survey argument. Especially helpful trended data spans multiple years

title: Add age and census age groups to a data frame — add_ages
text: 1. year_born_numeric 2. age_numeric 3. census_age_groups 4. census_age_groups_6 (census_age_groups, split 18-34 18-24 & 25-34)

title: Add age and census age groups to a data frame — add_ages
code: responses <- tibble::tibble(   year_born = c(   2000,   2001   ),   age = c(   20,   19   ) )  add_ages(responses, year_born_var = year_born) #> # A tibble: 2 × 6 #>   year_born   age year_born_numeric age_numeric census_age_groups #>       <dbl> <dbl>             <dbl>       <dbl> <chr>             #> 1      2000    20              2000          26 18-34             #> 2      2001    19              2001          25 18-34             #> # ℹ 1 more variable: census_age_groups_6 <chr> add_ages(responses, age_var = age) #> # A tibble: 2 × 6 #>   year_born   age age_numeric year_born_numeric census_age_groups #>       <dbl> <dbl>       <dbl>             <dbl> <chr>             #> 1      2000    20          20              2006 18-34             #> 2      2001    19          19              2007 18-34             #> # ℹ 1 more variable: census_age_groups_6 <chr>

title: Add a
text: Takes data frame state variable uses states categorize US census regions

title: Add a
code: add_regions(dataset, state_var, new_name = census_region)

title: Add a
text: dataset name data frame function modify, usually piped main data frame. state_var DEFAULT; state variable census regions based. Can state name state abbreviations new_name DEFAULT = census_region; name new census region variable, object "string"

title: Add a
code: responses <- tibble::tibble(   state = c(   'Virginia',   'UTAH',   'New York',   'alaska',   'Washington DC'   ) )  add_regions(responses, state_var = state) #> # A tibble: 5 × 2 #>   state         census_region #>   <chr>         <chr>         #> 1 Virginia      South         #> 2 UTAH          West          #> 3 New York      Northeast     #> 4 alaska        West          #> 5 Washington DC South

what: Order your data and add percent labels — order_label
title: Order your data and add percent labels — order_label
text: Takes dataframe (frequencies) orders labels groups adding percent labels use ggplot

title: Order your data and add percent labels — order_label
code: order_label(   dataset,   label_var = label,   group_var = "NULL",   inherent_order_label = FALSE,   inherent_order_group = FALSE,   label_first = NA,   group_first = NA,   rev_label = FALSE,   rev_group = FALSE,   label_last = NA,   group_last = NA,   horizontal = lifecycle::deprecated(),   direction = c("vertical", "horizontal"),   stacked = c("NULL", "ms", "gg"),   topbox = NULL,   none_other = TRUE,   num_fmt = c("percent", "general"),   percent_all = FALSE )

title: Order your data and add percent labels — order_label
text: dataset name data frame function modify, usually piped running freqs label_var DEFAULT = label; name variable ordered group_var DEFAULT = 'NULL'; Add unquoted name grouping variable data grouped inherent_order_label DEFAULT = FALSE; FALSE, puts labels descending order. TRUE, puts labels inherent order survey (e.g., Strongly agree strongly disagree). Specifying stacked = 'gg' 'ms' automatically makes inherent_order_label = TRUE inherent_order_group DEFAULT = FALSE; FALSE, puts groups descending order. TRUE, puts groups order factored (e.g., District 1, District 2...) label_first DEFAULT = NA; specified, puts specified label first. ex: 'brand1' put label called brand1 labels group_first DEFAULT = NA; specified, puts specified group first. ex: 'brand1' put group called brand1 groups rev_label DEFAULT = FALSE; reverse order labels chart, use rev_label = TRUE rev_group DEFAULT = FALSE; reverse order groups chart, use rev_group = TRUE label_last DEFAULT = NA; specified, puts specified label last ex: 'brand1' put label called brand1 labels group_last DEFAULT = NA; specified, puts specified group last ex: 'brand1' put group called brand1 groups horizontal (Deprecated) Use direction instead. direction DEFAULT = "vertical"; Use "vertical" (default) "horizontal". Replaces deprecated `horizontal` argument. stacked DEFAULT = 'NULL'; stacked barcharts, use stacked = 'gg' ggplot 'ms' mschart topbox DEFAULT = NULL; Can set numeric value, ex: topbox = 2 order top2box instead topbox none_other DEFAULT = TRUE; Automatically puts "", "None ", "Prefer say" options bottom. Change FALSE let stay ordered elsewhere chart num_fmt DEFAULT = "percent"; option "general", use working whole numbers rather percents/proportions percent_all DEFAULT = FALSE; FALSE, put % next first number label chart. set TRUE, put %s next numbers labels

title: Order your data and add percent labels — order_label
code: # Ungrouped, put in descending order of the result frequencies <- tibble::tibble(   label = c('Brand 1', 'Brand 2', 'Brand 3', 'Brand 4', 'Brand 5'),   result = c(.25, .15, .20, .10, .30),   value = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5) ) |> order_label()  # Grouped, with an inherent order for the label, or the brand frequencies <- tibble::tibble(   label = rep(c('Brand 1', 'Brand 2', 'Brand 3', 'Brand 4', 'Brand 5'), 2),   result = c(.20, .20, .30, .10, .20, .20, .30, .20, .20, .10),   value = rep(c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5), 2),   group_var = c(rep('Group A', 5), rep('Group B', 5)) ) |> order_label(   group_var = group_var,   inherent_order_label = TRUE )  # Stacked, will be using this frequencies in an mschart later on frequencies <- tibble::tibble(   label = rep(c('Promoter', 'Passive', 'Detractor'), 3),   result = c(.33, .33, .34, .20, .30, .50, .25, .50, .25),   value = rep(c(1, 2, 3), 3),   group_var = c(rep('Group A', 3), rep('Group B', 3), rep('Group C', 3)) ) |> order_label(   group_var = group_var,   stacked = 'ms' )

what: Auto order a freqs table to match another freqs table — order_same
title: Auto order a freqs table to match another freqs table — order_same
text: Takes dataframe (frequencies) orders order another frequencies table R environment called "ordered_df".

title: Auto order a freqs table to match another freqs table — order_same
code: order_same(dataset, orders = ordered_df, group_var = "NULL")

title: Auto order a freqs table to match another freqs table — order_same
text: dataset name data frame function modify, usually piped running freqs orders DEFAULT = ordered_df; First create ordered dataframe called ordered_df using order_label. new dataframe created using order_same "label" "group_var" columns order dataframe specified argument. group_var DEFAULT = 'NULL'. Leave default grouping variable grouping variable called group_var. Otherwise, specify group_var - quotes. specify variable name, variable converted new "group_var" column, data ordered column.

title: Auto order a freqs table to match another freqs table — order_same
code: # The original frequencies, put in descending order of the result frequencies <- tibble::tibble(   label = c('Brand 1', 'Brand 2', 'Brand 3', 'Brand 4', 'Brand 5'),   result = c(.25, .15, .20, .10, .30),   value = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5),   group_var = rep('Group A', 5) ) |> order_label(group_var = group_var) ordered_df <- frequencies  # The second frequencies that you want to be ordered the same as the original frequencies <- tibble::tibble(   label = c('Brand 1', 'Brand 2', 'Brand 3', 'Brand 4', 'Brand 5'),   result = c(.30, .10, .15, .20, .25),   value = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5),   group_var = rep('Group B', 5) ) |> order_same() #> Error in order_same(tibble::tibble(label = c("Brand 1", "Brand 2", "Brand 3",     "Brand 4", "Brand 5"), result = c(0.3, 0.1, 0.15, 0.2, 0.25),     value = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5), group_var = rep("Group B", 5))): object 'ordered_df' not found

what: Add %s to a topline report — order_topline
title: Add %s to a topline report — order_topline
text: Takes dataframe (frequencies) first Y (result) every X (variable), adds %. Also changes 0 <1 n >=1

title: Add %s to a topline report — order_topline
code: order_topline(   dataset,   whole_numbers = "place your variable names here with a | (OR sign) between them" )

title: Add %s to a topline report — order_topline
text: dataset name data frame mscharts pulls , usually piped running freqs. Please note variable column must "variable" percentage column must "result" whole_numbers DEFAULT = variables percentages, whole number variables, can leave argument blank. Otherwise, add names variables percentages separated "|" (sign). type whole variable name/option multiple select. unique portion var name work well argument uses str_detect().

title: Add %s to a topline report — order_topline
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ frequencies <- freqs(mtcars, vs, am, gear, carb) order_topline(frequencies)  frequencies <- freqs(mtcars, mpg, cyl, disp, stat = 'mean') order_topline(frequencies, 'mpg|cyl|disp') } # }

title: Auto change
text: Takes dataframe (frequencies) replaces usual variations "please specify" . Also standardizes "None " "Prefer say" options. Additionally, removes extra text parentheses opening. 'label', 'variable', 'group_var' vars.

title: Auto change
code: other_rm(dataset, var = NULL, remove = FALSE)

title: Auto change
text: dataset name data frame function modify, usually piped running freqs. almost never need arguments function. var DEFAULT = NULL; kept NULL, already remove parenthetical text 'label', 'variable', 'group_var' vars. can add additional variable specifying . remove DEFAULT = FALSE; set TRUE, remove categories "" "None " frequencies

title: Auto change
code: frequencies <- tibble::tibble(   label = c(   'Brand 1',   'Brand 2',   'Brand 3',   'Other (please specify)',   'None of the above brands...'   ),   result = c(.25, .15, .20, .10, .30),   other_var = c('x', 'y', 'z', 'z (test)', 'None') )  frequencies |> other_rm(remove = TRUE) #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>   label   result other_var #>   <chr>    <dbl> <chr>     #> 1 Brand 1   0.25 x         #> 2 Brand 2   0.15 y         #> 3 Brand 3   0.2  z         frequencies |> other_rm(var = other_var) #> # A tibble: 5 × 3 #>   label             result other_var         #>   <chr>              <dbl> <chr>             #> 1 Brand 1             0.25 x                 #> 2 Brand 2             0.15 y                 #> 3 Brand 3             0.2  z                 #> 4 Other               0.1  z                 #> 5 None of the above   0.3  None of the above

title: Pipe operator — %>%
text: See magrittr::%>% details.

title: Pipe operator — %>%
code: lhs %>% rhs

what: Auto remove question preambles for multiple choice/matrix questions — preamble_rm
title: Auto remove question preambles for multiple choice/matrix questions — preamble_rm
text: Takes dataframe (frequencies) removes usual question preambles left labels care . example, "favorite color? - Blue" becomes "Blue"

title: Auto remove question preambles for multiple choice/matrix questions — preamble_rm
code: preamble_rm(dataset, var = prompt, before_symbol = "- ")

title: Auto remove question preambles for multiple choice/matrix questions — preamble_rm
text: dataset name data frame function modify, usually piped running freqs. almost never need arguments function. var DEFAULT = prompt; use prompt = TRUE argument freqs(), default perfect . before_symbol DEFAULT = "- "; preamble_rm remove everything symbol string symbols

title: Auto remove question preambles for multiple choice/matrix questions — preamble_rm
code: frequencies <- tibble::tibble(   label = c('Selected','Selected','Selected','Selected','Selected'),   result = c(.25, .15, .20, .10, .30),   prompt = c(   'What is your favorite color? - Blue',   'What is your favorite color? - Green',   'What is your favorite color? - Yellow',   'What is your favorite color? - Purple',   'What is your favorite color? - Orange'   ),   prompt2 = c(   'What is your favorite color? ... Blue',   'What is your favorite color? ... Green',   'What is your favorite color? ... Yellow',   'What is your favorite color? ... Purple',   'What is your favorite color? ... Orange'   ) )  preamble_rm(frequencies) #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   label    result prompt prompt2                                 #>   <chr>     <dbl> <chr>  <chr>                                   #> 1 Selected   0.25 Blue   What is your favorite color? ... Blue   #> 2 Selected   0.15 Green  What is your favorite color? ... Green  #> 3 Selected   0.2  Yellow What is your favorite color? ... Yellow #> 4 Selected   0.1  Purple What is your favorite color? ... Purple #> 5 Selected   0.3  Orange What is your favorite color? ... Orange preamble_rm(frequencies, var = prompt2, before_symbol = '\\. ') #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   label    result prompt                                prompt2 #>   <chr>     <dbl> <chr>                                 <chr>   #> 1 Selected   0.25 What is your favorite color? - Blue   Blue    #> 2 Selected   0.15 What is your favorite color? - Green  Green   #> 3 Selected   0.2  What is your favorite color? - Yellow Yellow  #> 4 Selected   0.1  What is your favorite color? - Purple Purple  #> 5 Selected   0.3  What is your favorite color? - Orange Orange  frequencies |> preamble_rm() #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   label    result prompt prompt2                                 #>   <chr>     <dbl> <chr>  <chr>                                   #> 1 Selected   0.25 Blue   What is your favorite color? ... Blue   #> 2 Selected   0.15 Green  What is your favorite color? ... Green  #> 3 Selected   0.2  Yellow What is your favorite color? ... Yellow #> 4 Selected   0.1  Purple What is your favorite color? ... Purple #> 5 Selected   0.3  Orange What is your favorite color? ... Orange

what: Read multiple excel sheets at once — read_excel_allsheets
title: Read multiple excel sheets at once — read_excel_allsheets
text: Read multiple excel sheets workbook & turn one tibble series data frames named sheet name. excel sheet matching column names ensure correct row binding occurs.

title: Read multiple excel sheets at once — read_excel_allsheets
code: read_excel_allsheets(filename, single_frame = TRUE)

title: Read multiple excel sheets at once — read_excel_allsheets
text: filename file path .xlsx want pull sheets single_frame DEFAULT = TRUE; TRUE, combine sheets excel workbook single data frame variable called "sheet_name" values different sheet names. FALSE, read sheets excel workbook create separate data frame sheet (data frame named sheet called)

what: Create a dataframe of varaible names and labels — taking_names
title: Create a dataframe of varaible names and labels — taking_names
text: Takes dataset (responses) creates new df two columns: 1) variable names original dataset, 2) labels variables (question wording sav/rds file)

title: Create a dataframe of varaible names and labels — taking_names
code: taking_names(dataset = responses)

title: Create a dataframe of varaible names and labels — taking_names
text: dataset DEFAULT = responses; name data frame function use, typically responses

title: Create a dataframe of varaible names and labels — taking_names
code: names <- taking_names(mtcars)  responses <- mtcars names <- taking_names() #> Error in taking_names(): object 'responses' not found

title: orderlabel 0.4.3
text: horizontal argument order_label() now deprecated, replaced direction.

title: orderlabel 0.4.2
text: order_label - new error messages added, documentation updated ## Bug fixes order_label - fixed bugs rev_label ungrouped data ## Tests order_label - Test suite redone match tidy testing best practices: tests written use case private function performance. Expanded tests beyond current coverage

title: orderlabel 0.4.1
text: order_label - fixed bugs around rev_group always working arguments

title: orderlabel 0.4.0
text: order_same - group_var argument now takes variables outside quotes instead inside quotes. Done way match formatting order_label tidy functions. ## New features order_label - new argument percent_all DEFAULT = FALSE; FALSE, put % next first number label chart (order_label always previously functioned). set TRUE, put %s next numbers labels. other_rm - new argument var. Previously other_rm always removed parenthetical text variables ‘label,’ ‘variable,’ ‘group_var.’ Now can specify additional variable var argument. ## Bug fixes order_same - fixed problems group_var argument sometimes working expected other_rm preamble_rm - robust removing edge cases extra types white space

title: orderlabel 0.3.4
text: add_ages() - New argument: survey_date_var date variable dataset specifies survey taken. specified, ages calculated year_of_survey argument. Especially helpful trended data spans multiple years. example, argument calculate someone born 2000 taking survey 2020 20 took survey. However, respondent took another wave survey 2021, now calculated 21 years old new round still 20 years old previous round.

title: orderlabel 0.3.3
text: add_ages() - New argument: “year_of_survey”. DEFAULT = Whatever current year . Used calculate respondent ages. using old survey data previous year, can input year numeric value (e.g. 1994)

title: orderlabel 0.3.1
text: add_ages() - census_age_groups now ordered factor variable instead character variable. Function also now creates “census_age_groups_6” (original census_age_groups, 18-34 group split 18-24 & 25-34) ## Bugs order_label() - Problem fixed order_label() sometimes break freqs grouped user-created variables

title: orderlabel 0.3.0
text: order_label() - TLDR: Order rows output now arranged factor level, order_label now works updated versions htmltools mschart. Details … update fixes ordering problems introduced htmltools versions > 3.6.0 mschart versions > 2.5.0. , frequency output order_label may look slightly different cases. label/group_var still factored order , physical arrangement different cases. mschart now pulls “physical order” tables arranged , “factor order”, frequency table look different cases match mschart, charts ordered correctly, regardless run mschart ggplot2.

title: orderlabel 0.2.3
text: topline() function name changed order_topline() y2_municipal package function called topline now. Shouldn’t problem bet one used function anyway. developers didn’t even use ;)

title: orderlabel 0.2.3
text: New error kept occuring trying use topbox argument order_label, error now fixed!

title: orderlabel 0.2.2
text: examples now working Fixed strange error showing cases: “length condition > 1” Eliminated notes missing global variables

title: orderlabel 0.2.1
text: order_label label_secific group_specific replaced label_first group_first. Naming conventions changed due additions label_last group_last.

title: orderlabel 0.2.1
text: order_label New arguments: label_last group_last. can use arguments choose specific label group level want show last chart

title: orderlabel 0.1.6
text: NEWS.md README.md cran-comments.md

title: orderlabel 0.1.6
text: none_other argument order_label now applies group_var, just label Solidified T F TRUE FALSE (help minor bugs) Eliminated lots function notes: missing “global variables” “global functions” Updated examples documentation

title: orderlabel 0.1.5
text: Now works “variable” “group_var” (addition “label”) “remove” argument added option create one multiple data frames var indicate sheet names User may specify grouping variable Now creates tibble num_fmt argument added Reprex examples functions

title: orderlabel 0.1.5
text: preamble_rm ever gotten freqs matrix questions multiple choice questions get stuck long question wording attached prompt like “favorite color - Blue”? want “Blue”? Well function ! automatically removes pesky question wording. add_regions Creates census_region variable based state variable give . add_ages Give year born variable age variable (can still original Qualtrics, factor labelled form), create three new variables 1. year_born_numeric, 2. age_numeric, 3. census_age_groups (using census age groups set municipal surveys)

title: orderlabel 0.1.0
text: First official release GitHub
